note: Golden fixture for the deterministic Park-Miller label stream: seed 42.0, three draws
per row (adjective, colour, noun), 25/11/13 word lists. The LCG is seeded ONCE per page
load and is never reset per #run click, so the Nth #run on a page emits a DIFFERENT
firstRun  = the first #run on a freshly loaded page (LCG draws 1..3000, ids 1..1000).
secondRun = the second #run on the SAME page (LCG draws 3001..6000, ids 1001..2000).
This is what create-warm times, and create-warm is C4's headline.
Both are asserted by verifyContract. Gating only firstRun would leave the headline
number ungated: an app that runs the real LCG once and reuses the interned strings on
every later #run passes a first-run-only gate byte-exactly, then does ZERO of the 3000
multiply/modulo ops and ZERO of the 1000 three-part concatenations on the run that is
actually timed. The two streams below MUST differ, and loadExpectedLabels refuses a
fixture in which they do not.
